research_md:
# Research

## Product Idea

A personal expense tracker web app that supports income and expense entry, categories, and monthly statistics.

## Sources Or Assumptions

- Assumption: This local manual Codex flow did not browse the web, so product claims are treated as assumptions until PM research tools are connected.
- Assumption: The first user is a solo personal finance user who values fast entry over advanced reporting.
- Assumption: The painful current alternative is a spreadsheet or notes app that is fast to edit but weak for trustworthy monthly review.
- Source: Local project brief from the user.

## Insights

- The MVP should prioritize quick transaction capture because repeated manual entry is the highest-friction workflow.
- Categories should be editable during entry and visible in summaries.
- Monthly totals should show income, expenses, and net balance because those are the core personal budget questions.
- The valuable product artifact is an inspectable monthly cash-flow summary showing income, expenses, and net total from source transactions.
- Repeat use comes from adding transactions throughout the month and reviewing the monthly summary before budget decisions.

## Evidence Chain

- Source evidence -> insight: monthly review matters -> PRD decision: keep monthly totals in MVP -> QA gate: verify totals after transaction changes.

## Risks

- If entry is too slow, users will stop maintaining the tracker.
- If categories are too rigid, users will work around them or abandon categorization.
competitor_matrix_md:
# Competitor Matrix

| Competitor / Reference | Source | Pattern | Opportunity | Caution |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Spreadsheet workflow | Source | Flexible manual tracking. | Faster guided entry and monthly summaries. | Too much freedom can become inconsistent data. |
| Budgeting app | Source | Monthly category review drives value. | Keep income, expense, and net totals prominent. | Bank sync is out of MVP scope. |
| Notes app | Assumption | Fast capture is easy but hard to summarize. | Make entry nearly as fast while preserving structure. | Notes do not scale to reporting. |
| Dashboard app | Assumption | Summary surfaces motivate review. | Make summaries traceable to source transactions. | Avoid dashboard bloat. |

## Product Takeaway

A good MVP should compete on fast capture plus trustworthy monthly review, not on finance-platform breadth.
pm_debate_md:
# PM Debate

## Market PM

The market expectation is fast transaction capture and reliable monthly review.

## UX Researcher

The critical journey is add transaction, categorize it, and verify monthly totals.

## Product Designer

The UI should be calm, dense, and optimized for repeated entry rather than a marketing-style dashboard.

## Technical PM

The MVP is feasible if it stays local-first with transaction CRUD, validation, and deterministic summaries.

## Visual/AI PM

AI visuals are not needed for MVP; the product should prioritize clarity and trustworthy totals.

## Critic

The main failure mode is generic CRUD without a useful monthly review artifact.

## Decision

Proceed with a narrow local expense tracker focused on fast entry and inspectable monthly summaries.
prd_md:
# Product Requirements

## Background

The product is a local personal expense tracker web app. It helps a single user add income and expenses, organize transactions by category, and review monthly financial totals.

## Product Strategy And Differentiation

The product is narrower than a full finance suite because it focuses on fast local entry and monthly review.
It should beat spreadsheets by producing a trustworthy monthly cash-flow artifact without bank sync or finance-platform setup.

## Product Management Operating Model

- Aha!-style lifecycle keeps strategy, MVP, non-goals, and delivery connected.
- Productboard-style traceability keeps each MVP feature tied to user value.

## Users

- A solo user who wants a private, local-first way to track spending.
- A budget-conscious user who wants quick monthly visibility without connecting bank accounts.

## Core Problem

The user pain is that spreadsheets and notes are quick but make monthly income, expense, and net-total review manual and error-prone.
The user needs a lightweight way to record transactions and produce an inspectable monthly cash-flow summary without using a full finance platform.

## MVP

- Add income and expense transactions.
- Capture amount, date, type, category, and optional note.
- Edit and delete transactions.
- View a monthly summary with income, expenses, and net total.
- Produce an inspectable monthly cash-flow summary artifact.
- Filter or group transactions by category.

## V1

- CSV export.
- Recurring transaction templates.
- Category management.

## Non-goals

- Bank synchronization.
- Multi-user collaboration.
- Cloud deployment.
- Tax reporting.

## Risks

- Manual entry may be tedious if the form has too many fields.
- Monthly statistics may be misleading if transaction type or date validation is weak.
- Scope could expand into full accounting unless non-goals are enforced.
user_stories_md:
# User Stories

- As a budget-conscious user, I want to add an expense quickly, so that I can keep my spending record current.
- As a user with variable income, I want to add income transactions, so that my monthly net total is accurate.
- As a user, I want to categorize transactions, so that I can understand where money is going.
- As a user, I want to view monthly income, expenses, and net total, so that I can review my financial position.
- As a user, I want to edit or delete mistakes, so that my records stay accurate.
acceptance_criteria_md:
# Acceptance Criteria

- Given I am on the transaction form, when I enter a valid expense with amount, date, and category, then the transaction appears in the list as an expense.
- Given I am on the transaction form, when I enter a valid income item, then the transaction appears in the list as income.
- Given a transaction exists, when I edit the category and save, then the updated category appears in the transaction list and monthly summary.
- Given a transaction exists, when I delete it, then it is removed from the list and monthly totals update.
- Given a month contains income and expenses, when I open monthly statistics, then income total, expense total, and net total are shown.
scope_md:
# Scope

## MVP

- Local web app for personal finance tracking.
- Transaction CRUD for income and expenses.
- Required fields: amount, date, type, category.
- Optional field: note.
- Monthly statistics: income, expenses, net total.
- Inspectable monthly cash-flow summary artifact.
- Basic category grouping.

## V1

- CSV import/export.
- Recurring transaction templates.
- Category management UI.
- Simple trend chart.

## Future

- Budget targets.
- Rule-based categorization.
- Receipt attachment.
- Multi-device sync.

## Non-goals

- Bank sync.
- Cloud account system.
- Multi-user sharing.
- Tax filing workflows.
prd_quality_score_md:
# PRD Quality Score

- Research depth: 8/10
- Differentiation: 8/10
- UX specificity: 8/10
- Visual strategy: 7/10
- Feasibility: 8/10
- Testability: 9/10

Final score: 48/60
Status: pass
